rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF EIVEN E. PANTOJA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/12/2023 for the following additional issues to be addressed: 1) The court-appointed attorney for the conservatee has not petitioned for fees/costs and discharge. Does she intend to request any payment from the county or cash on hand of the estate? If so, the petition must be filed and adjudicated before an order terminating this conservatorship is issued. 2) Notice of the amendment was given on 05/14/2023, before the amendment was filed on 05/17/2023. See LRSF 14.14(A). 3) Has Rena Harel (former conservator) agreed to waive the balance of her fees ($32,718.50) in exchange for the $40,000 payment? 4) The $15,318.96 fees of the attorney for Rena Harel approved by the 2018 was not included in prorating the cash on hand. 5) Based on the assessment orders actually issued, the total amount of unpaid assessments is $3,600. No assessment order appears to have been issued for the 09/2011 & 2022 investigations.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANDREW GEORGE JAMESON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/18/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No courtesy copies have been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] (3) No proof of service of Notice of Hearing has been filed. (Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.]) (4) If original financial institution statements have not been filed, they must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. Deadline is 7/7/23 for delivering the proposed order and endorsed filed courtesy copies of the petition, proof of service, and all other supporting papers to the assigned examiner. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE FRIDA KOPPL TRUST ONE DATED 8/5/11 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To (1) Special Interrogatories, Set Three And (2) Form Interrogatories, Set Three; And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 4] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the court is Rudolph Koppl's Motion to Compel Further Responses To (1) Special Interrogatories, Set Three And (2) Form Interrogatories, Set Three; And For Monetary Sanctions. Respondents Monica Zimmerman and Alan Zimmerman's Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED. Judicial notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) Respondents Monica Zimmerman and Alan Zimmerman lodged objections to and seek to strike as inadmissible certain portions of the moving papers. The court rules on the evidentiary objections to each "specification," as termed by Respondents, as follows: SPECIFICATION No. 1, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 5:6-8. OBJECTION: Lack of Foundation as to personal knowledge. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 2, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 5:11. OBJECTION: OBJECTION: Incompetent, Conclusory. Lack of foundation as to personal knowledge. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 3, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 6:26-27. OBJECTION: Failure to establish preliminary facts. OVERRULED. OBJECTION: Incompetent, Conclusory. Lack of foundation as to personal knowledge. OVERRULED. OBJECTION: Improper Testimony Regarding Contents of a Writing. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 4, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 5:15. OBJECTION: Incompetent, Conclusory. Lack of foundation as to personal knowledge. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 5, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 5:15-16. OBJECTION: OBJECTION: Failure to establish preliminary facts. OVERRULED. OBJECTION: Incompetent, Conclusory. Lack of foundation as to personal knowledge. OVERRULED. OBJECTION: Improper Testimony Regarding Contents of a Writing. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 6, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 5:17. OBJECTION: Conclusory, Speculative. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 7, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 5:17-18. OBJECTION: Incompetent, Conclusory. Lack of foundation as to personal knowledge. OVERRULED. OBJECTION: Irrelevant and Prejudicial. Evid. Code sections 210, 350, 352. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 8, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 5:18-19. OBJECTION: Irrelevant and Prejudicial. Evid. Code sections 210, 350, 352. OVERRULED. OBJECTION: Lack of Foundation. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 9, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 5:23-24. OBJECTION: Incompetent, Conclusory. Lack of foundation as to personal knowledge. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 10, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 5:24-26. OBJECTION: Incompetent, Conclusory. Lack of foundation as to personal knowledge. OVERRULED. SPECIFICATION No. 11, Rudolph's Separate Statement, 6:17-18. OBJECTION: Lack of supporting authority. OVERRULED. [Part 1 of 4]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE FRIDA KOPPL TRUST ONE DATED 8/5/11 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion To Compel Further Responses To (1) Special Interrogatories, Set Three And (2) Form Interrogatories, Set Three; And For Monetary Sanctions (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 4 of 4] Rudolph Koppl's Special Interrogatories to Monica Zimmerman, Set Three ("SI") Nos. 103 through 111, served on February 8, 2023, pursuant to Code Civ. Proc. section1 2030.300 and (2) Rudolph Koppl's Form Interrogatories to Monica Zimmerman, Set Three ("FI") Nos. 1.1 and 17.1(b)-(d), served on February 8, 2023, pursuant to Code Civ. Proc. section 2030.300, seek relevant, admissible evidence, or matter than appears reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence, in the form of facts, documents, and identity of witnesses, concerning Monica's contentions regarding the validity, applicability, and effect of a handwritten document dated November 14, 2012, on the Trust and related testamentary instruments. The interrogatories at issue are relevant to the subject matter of the action. Petitioner and Respondent Beneficiary Rudolph Koppl's Motion to Compel Further Responses To (1) Special Interrogatories and (2) Form interrogatories; and for Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED. Further Responses shall be served no later than June 30, 2023. Petitioner and Respondent Beneficiary Rudolph Koppl's request for sanctions is GRANTED pursuant to Code of Civ. Proc. section 2030.300(d). The court does not find that Respondents Monica and Alan Zimmerman acted with substantial justification in opposing this motion to compel or that other circumstances make the imposition of sanctions unjust. Based on all of the evidence before the Court, Monica and Alan Zimmerman are ordered to pay Rudolph Koppl sanctions on this motion in the amount of five thousand dollars ($5,000), within 10 days of service of notice of entry of this order. Moving party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with all substantive provisions of this tentative ruling, as published above, by the time set for this hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 4 of 4]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE FRIDA KOPPL TRUST ONE DATED 8/5/11 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion To Compel Further Responses To (1) Special Interrogatories, Set Three And (2) Form Interrogatories, Set Three; And For Monetary Sanctions (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 3 of 4] Rudolph seeks an order compelling Respondent Monica Zimmerman ("Monica") to provide further responses to (1) Rudolph Koppl's Special Interrogatories to Monica Zimmerman, Set Three ("SI") Nos. 103 through 111, served on February 8, 2023, pursuant to Code Civ. Proc. section1 2030.300 and (2) Rudolph Koppl's Form Interrogatories to Monica Zimmerman, Set Three ("FI") Nos. 1.1 and 17.1(b)-(d), served on February 8, 2023, pursuant to Code Civ. Proc. section 2030.300, on the grounds that the interrogatories seek information, not privileged, that is relevant to the subject matter of the action, and Monica's objections thereto are without merit or too general. The motion further seeks an award of monetary sanctions in the amount of $5,000 pursuant to Code of Civ. Proc. sections 2023.030 and 2030.300(d). Monica's responses to the Special and Form Interrogatories at issue are entirely in the form of various objections, with no substantive responses. Any party to an action may obtain discovery regarding any matter not privileged that is relevant to the subject matter involved in the pending action, if the matter either is itself admissible in evidence or appears reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence, unless otherwise limited by court order in accordance with the Discovery Act. (Code of Civil Proc. section 2017.010). Discovery may relate to the claim or defense of the party seeking discovery, or of any other party to the action. (Code of Civil Proc. section 2017.010.) When a motion to compel discovery is brought, the burden generally rests upon the party seeking the discovery to provide evidence from which the court may determine the subject of the discovery motion "is itself admissible in evidence or appears reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence." (Calcor Space Facility, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1997) 53 Cal. App. 4th 216, 223.) California's discovery statutes "must be construed liberally in favor of disclosure unless the request is clearly improper by virtue of well-established causes for denial." (Greyhound Corp. v Sup. Ct. (1961) 56 Cal.2d 355, 377.) Any doubts "should generally be resolved in favor of permitting discovery." (Williams v Sup. Ct. (2017) 3 Cal.5th 531, 542 quoting Pacific Tel. & Tel. Co. v Sup. Ct. (1970) 2 Cal.3d 161, 173.) Rudolph has filed "Amended Petition to Interpret Trust." Monica and Alan Zimmerman have filed "Petition to Determine Existence of Nonexistence Of Power, Duty, Or Right; To Determine to Whom Property Shall Pass Or Be Delivered; And To Pass Upon The Acts Of Frida Koppl, As Then Trustee." Both petitions are filed in connection with the Frida Koppl Trust One dated August 5, 2011 (the "Trust"). At issue in both petitions is the validity, applicability, and effect of a document dated November 14, 2012, purportedly handwritten and signed by Decedent Frida Koppl, on the Trust and related testamentary instruments. Monica's Petition seeks a determination that "the 2012 Amendment was and is a valid amendment to [the Trust]" (11/27/18 Petition, 7:9-17). Rudolph's Amended Petition prays "for a ruling that the 2012 Handwriting was not a valid amendment to [the Trust]" (1/4/19 Amended Petition, 12:10-14). [Part 3 of 4]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALEKSANDER ALEKSANDROVICH DONSKOY AKA ALEKSANDER ALEKSANDROVICH DONSKOY AKA DONSKOI ALEKSANDER ALEKSAND
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/12/2023 (no earlier date available). 1) No courtesy copy of the petition has been received (as of 06/12/2023). 2) Paragraphs 8, 9, 10(i), 13 and 14 of the declaration filed on 06/02/2023 must be verified by the petitioner, unless the attorney's verification includes language required by Probate Code section 1023 & CRC 7.103(c). 3) No proposed order (hard copy) has been received (as of 06/12/2023). 4) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) and attached to the DE-120 form listed an incorrect telephone number for a Zoom appearance. 5) No allegation has been made as to whether any property was owned by the decedent before marriage, acquired by gift or inheritance, or purchased with funds received by gift or inheritance. LRSF 14.26(A). 6) A copy of a membership/share certificate or other documentation showing how the decedent held title to each business interest (5% LLC membership and corporate shares) has not been filed. Did the decedent hold title in his individual name? It is also unclear how many shares of stock of Big Funny Corp the decedent owned. 7) The application of Article 1149 of the Russian Civil Code re a forced statutory share of each minor child of the decedent (see the decedent's will admitted to probate) must be addressed, including whether and how the shares must be satisfied from this ancillary estate.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GERALD PLOTKIN LIVING TRUST, DATED OCTOBER 30, 2001
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Amend Trust Due To Scriveners Error, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, And Declarations Of Victoria S. Bahnasy And Diane Green In
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1. Petition to allege the heirs of Dina Barauskas or the personal representative of Dina's estate, and all of them need to be noticed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DOROTHY ANNE MARTIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DONALD ISIAH HUGHES
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/26/23. The following procedural issues need to be cleared two weeks prior: (1) Notice of Petition to Administer Estate must set forth an amended petition being filed. CRC 7.53(a) (2) Proof of publication showing Amended Petition being filed. PC 8120 and CRC 7.54. (3) Verified supplemental declaration suggesting full bond amount ($611,000), not $20,000. Proposed administrator seeks Full IAEA and no Waiver of Bond being on file as Petition #3.e. silent. (4) Need Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number [JC Form DE-147S] to be filed. LRSF 14.18.E. (5) Written declinations by other heirs. Otherwise, Petition to indicate the reason if no written document is on file. LRSF 14.24 (6) Proof of notice of direction for remote appearances to all persons entitled. LRSF 14.1.D.6) (7) Proposed Order to be submitted prior to the hearing. LRSF 14.7.A.
